query:
UPDATE blog_posts SET 
  title = 'Why Men Are Ditching Malls for Online Thrift',
  excerpt = 'The modern man knows what he wants. No more wandering aimlessly through department stores or waiting outside fitting rooms. Here''s why smart shoppers are going digital.',
  content = '# Why Men Are Ditching Malls for Online Thrift

Let''s be honest - most men don''t enjoy shopping. The crowds, the fluorescent lighting, the endless racks of clothes that all look the same. And if you''ve ever been dragged to a mall by a partner, you know the drill: stand around, give opinions on things you don''t care about, and eventually end up buying something just to escape.

## Take Back Control

Urban Refit changes the game. Browse from your couch, during your lunch break, or at 2 AM when you can''t sleep. No pressure, no salespeople hovering, no one asking "can I help you find something?"

### What Real Men Want

- **Quality brands** - Levi''s, Carhartt, Dickies, Ralph Lauren. Names you trust.
- **Clear sizing** - Every measurement listed. No guessing.
- **Real photos** - What you see is what you get. No surprises.
- **Fast checkout** - In and out in under 2 minutes.

## The Numbers Don''t Lie

Men spend an average of 26 minutes per shopping trip when forced to go in-store. Online? 8 minutes to find what you need and check out. That''s 18 minutes back in your life, every single time.

## One-of-a-Kind Pieces

Here''s the thing about secondhand - every item is unique. That vintage Carhartt jacket? There''s only one. When it''s gone, it''s gone. No waiting for restocks, no "out of your size." You see it, you want it, you get it.

## Built for Men Who Know What They Want

Urban Refit isn''t about browsing for hours. It''s about finding quality pieces, checking the specs, and making a decision. Simple. Efficient. Done.

Stop letting someone else pick your clothes. Your style, your choice, your time.',
  category = 'style_tips',
  tags = '["menswear", "online shopping", "efficiency", "lifestyle"]'
WHERE id = 1;

UPDATE blog_posts SET 
  title = 'The Working Man''s Guide to Building a Capsule Wardrobe',
  excerpt = 'Forget trends. Forget fast fashion. Here''s how to build a wardrobe that works as hard as you do - with pieces that last.',
  content = '# The Working Man''s Guide to Building a Capsule Wardrobe

You don''t need 50 shirts. You need 10 good ones. The capsule wardrobe isn''t about minimalism for its own sake - it''s about having the right tools for the job.

## The Foundation: 15 Pieces That Do Everything

### Tops (6 pieces)
1. **White Oxford Shirt** - Meetings, dates, or rolled up for yard work
2. **Navy Polo** - The universal smart-casual option
3. **Grey Crewneck Sweatshirt** - Weekend essential
4. **Black T-Shirt** - Goes with everything
5. **Chambray Shirt** - Dressed up or down
6. **Flannel Shirt** - Layering champion

### Bottoms (4 pieces)
1. **Dark Indigo Jeans** - Levi''s 501 or similar
2. **Khaki Chinos** - Office-appropriate
3. **Black Jeans** - Evening ready
4. **Work Pants** - Dickies 874 never fails

### Outerwear (3 pieces)
1. **Denim Jacket** - Spring and fall staple
2. **Work Jacket** - Carhartt Detroit or similar
3. **Bomber Jacket** - Clean lines, no fuss

### Footwear (2 pieces)
1. **White Leather Sneakers** - Clean, versatile
2. **Brown Leather Boots** - Built to last

## Why Secondhand Makes Sense

New clothes are a gamble. Will they hold up? Will they fade? With pre-owned pieces, the answer is already there. If a Carhartt jacket has survived 5 years of someone else''s work, it''ll survive yours.

## Quality Over Quantity

One well-made jacket beats five cheap ones. Every time. The math is simple: spend $80 on a vintage Carhartt that lasts a decade, or $40 every year on something that falls apart.

## The Bottom Line

Build once. Maintain. Replace only when necessary. That''s how men have dressed for generations. No trends, no seasons - just clothes that work.',
  category = 'style_tips',
  tags = '["capsule wardrobe", "menswear", "workwear", "essentials"]'
WHERE id = 2;

UPDATE blog_posts SET 
  title = 'Brand Spotlight: Levi''s 501 - The Only Jeans You Need',
  excerpt = 'Since 1873, one pair of jeans has outlasted every trend, every competitor, and every attempt to improve on perfection. Here''s why the 501 is still king.',
  content = '# Brand Spotlight: Levi''s 501 - The Only Jeans You Need

Some things don''t need improvement. The 501 is one of them.

## 150 Years of Getting It Right

When Levi Strauss patented the riveted denim pant in 1873, he wasn''t thinking about fashion. He was thinking about miners who needed pants that wouldn''t fall apart. That focus on function over form is exactly why the 501 still works today.

## The Specs That Matter

- **Fit**: Straight leg, sits at waist
- **Rise**: Regular (not too high, not too low)
- **Button fly**: Slower to put on, but never breaks
- **Weight**: 12.5 oz denim - heavy enough to last, light enough to move

## Why Vintage 501s Are Better

Modern 501s are good. Vintage 501s are better. Here''s why:

### The Denim
Pre-1980s 501s used selvedge denim woven on shuttle looms. Denser, more durable, and they fade beautifully over time.

### The Construction
Older pairs were made in the USA with chain-stitch hems and reinforced stress points. Built to work, not to sell.

### The Character
A pair of 501s that''s been worn and washed for years has a character that new jeans can''t replicate. The fades tell a story.

## Finding Your Pair

At Urban Refit, we source vintage and pre-owned 501s in various conditions:
- **Like New**: Minimal wear, deep indigo
- **Excellent**: Light fading, broken in
- **Good**: Character fades, soft hand feel

## Size Guide

Vintage Levi''s run different from modern. Here''s the rule: buy your actual waist size. A 32" waist means a 32" jean. None of this vanity sizing nonsense.

## The Investment

A quality pair of vintage 501s will outlast anything you can buy new today. Period.',
  category = 'brand_spotlight',
  tags = '["levis", "501", "denim", "heritage", "menswear"]'
WHERE id = 3;

UPDATE blog_posts SET 
  title = 'From Thrift Store to Your Door: How We Source Quality Menswear',
  excerpt = 'Every piece in our collection is hand-picked by people who know what they''re looking for. Here''s how we find the good stuff.',
  content = '# From Thrift Store to Your Door: How We Source Quality Menswear

Not all thrift stores are created equal. And not all secondhand clothes are worth your time. Here''s how we separate the wheat from the chaff.

## The Selection Process

### Step 1: Partner Stores
We work with thrift stores that understand quality. These aren''t donation bins - they''re curated shops run by people who know the difference between a real Carhartt and a knockoff.

### Step 2: Brand Check
We focus on brands with proven track records:
- **Workwear**: Carhartt, Dickies, Red Kap
- **Denim**: Levi''s, Wrangler, Lee
- **Casual**: Ralph Lauren, Tommy Hilfiger, Nautica
- **Footwear**: Nike, New Balance, Red Wing

### Step 3: Condition Assessment
Every item is inspected for:
- Structural integrity (seams, zippers, buttons)
- Fabric condition (no holes, minimal pilling)
- Cleanliness (professionally cleaned before listing)
- Authenticity (no fakes, no knockoffs)

### Step 4: Honest Photography
We photograph every item on a neutral background. No filters, no tricks. What you see is what you get.

## The 10% Promise

For every sale, 10% goes back to the thrift store that sourced the item. It''s how we keep the pipeline flowing and support local communities.

## Why This Matters

Fast fashion is destroying the planet. 92 million tons of textile waste every year. By buying secondhand, you''re not just getting better quality - you''re keeping clothes out of landfills.

## The Result

Quality menswear, fairly priced, delivered to your door. No mall required.',
  category = 'behind_the_scenes',
  tags = '["sourcing", "quality", "sustainability", "process"]'
WHERE id = 4;

UPDATE blog_posts SET 
  title = '2026 Menswear Trends: What''s Actually Worth Buying',
  excerpt = 'Trends come and go. Here''s what''s worth your money this year - and what you should skip.',
  content = '# 2026 Menswear Trends: What''s Actually Worth Buying

Every year, fashion magazines tell you to throw out your wardrobe and start over. Don''t listen. Here''s what actually matters in 2026.

## Worth Your Money

### Workwear Renaissance
Carhartt, Dickies, and work-inspired pieces are everywhere. The difference? These brands have been making quality clothes for a century. The "trend" is just everyone else catching up.

**Buy**: Vintage Carhartt jackets, Dickies 874 pants, work boots
**Skip**: Fashion brand "workwear inspired" pieces at 3x the price

### Relaxed Fit Denim
Skinny jeans are done. Straight and relaxed fits are back - which is great news because that''s how jeans were meant to fit anyway.

**Buy**: Levi''s 501, 505, or vintage relaxed fits
**Skip**: Designer "wide leg" jeans at $300

### Earth Tones
Brown, olive, tan, rust. Colors that don''t require you to rebuild your wardrobe every season.

**Buy**: Neutral pieces that work with what you already own
**Skip**: Neon anything

## Skip Entirely

### Oversized Everything
There''s relaxed fit, and there''s wearing a tent. Know the difference.

### LogoMania
If you''re paying to be a walking billboard, you''re doing it wrong.

### "Sustainable" Fast Fashion
A $20 "eco-friendly" t-shirt from a fast fashion brand is still going to fall apart in 6 months. Buy quality secondhand instead.

## The Bottom Line

Real style doesn''t follow trends. It finds quality pieces and wears them for years. That vintage Carhartt jacket will still look good in 2030. Can''t say the same for whatever''s on the runway right now.',
  category = 'trends',
  tags = '["trends", "2026", "menswear", "workwear", "buying guide"]'
WHERE id = 5;
